rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Comparative co-expression analysis with rcomplex

Coarsen a module partition to fewer modules

Source: R/modules.R
coarsen_modules.Rd

Merges modules in a fine-grained partition to a target count by hierarchical clustering on inter-module edge density. This produces matched-granularity partitions for fair cross-species comparison when species have intrinsically different modular structure.

coarsen_modules(modules, target_n_modules)

Arguments

modules

Output of detect_modules().

target_n_modules

Integer, desired number of modules after coarsening. Must be at least 1 and less than the current number of modules.

Value

A list compatible with detect_modules() output (usable directly with compare_modules()), with additional components:

merge_map

Data frame with columns original_module and coarsened_module mapping old to new module IDs.

merge_dendrogram

The hclust object used for merging.

Algorithm

  1. Compute inter-module edge density for all module pairs: n_edges(i,j) / (size_i * size_j).

  2. Hierarchical clustering (complete linkage) on distance 1 - D / max(D).

  3. Cut the dendrogram at target_n_modules.

  4. Relabel module assignments contiguously and recompute module gene lists, modularity, and sizes.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
mods <- detect_modules(net, resolution = seq(0.25, 2.5, by = 0.25))
mods$n_modules  # 52
coarse <- coarsen_modules(mods, target_n_modules = 15)
coarse$n_modules  # 15
} # }
